text: The surgeon uses a Proline-to-O suture on a straight needle to string multiple fibroids together during a laparoscopic myomectomy, ensuring none are lost during the procedure.

The surgeon uses a laparoscopic tenaculum to hold multiple fibroids securely. The suture is then brought out of the abdomen, ensuring the fibroids remain in place during subsequent tasks such as suturing.
The surgeon ties the sutures around the fibroids to secure them, ensuring none are lost during removal. The fibroids are placed in a containment bag and removed using power morcellation.
